data.title: Rhythm Algorithm Aims To Clarify Bardet Biedl Diagnosis And Market Potential
data.description:
• Rhythm Pharmaceuticals (NasdaqGM:RYTM) has published a new evidence-based diagnostic algorithm for Bardet-Biedl syndrome.
  • The algorithm is designed to streamline and accelerate clinical diagnosis of this rare obesity disorder.
  • The update incorporates recent advances in genetic testing to support earlier and more accurate identification of BBS.

Rhythm Pharmaceuticals focuses on treatments for rare genetic forms of obesity, and Bardet-Biedl syndrome is a core part of that remit. A clearer diagnostic pathway can help clinicians identify eligible patients more efficiently, which is important in a segment where awareness, testing access, and patient identification often create bottlenecks. For investors following rare disease drug developers, diagnostic tools can matter almost as much as the therapies themselves because they influence how many patients are actually reached.

The new algorithm may support wider clinical adoption of BBS testing protocols and could influence how care teams screen patients with severe early-onset obesity. For Rhythm Pharmaceuticals, better diagnosis can shape how its therapies are used in practice and how healthcare systems think about rare obesity disorders over time.

Stay updated on the most important news stories for Rhythm Pharmaceuticals by adding it to your watchlist or portfolio. Alternatively, explore our Community to discover new perspectives on Rhythm Pharmaceuticals.

3 things going right for Rhythm Pharmaceuticals that this headline doesn't cover.

This publication sits at the intersection of medical practice and Rhythm Pharmaceuticals’ commercial opportunity. Bardet Biedl syndrome is hard to recognize, with symptoms that show up differently over time and across organs, which can delay diagnosis for years. By funding and coordinating an evidence based, consensus algorithm with international experts and advocacy groups, Rhythm is trying to remove some of that friction. A clearer clinical pathway, paired with guidance on when to use genetic testing, can help physicians identify patients earlier and more consistently across age groups. For a company focused on rare genetic obesity, that kind of diagnostic clarity is closely tied to how large and how accessible the real-world patient pool becomes. Publication in a peer reviewed genetics journal and a dedicated presentation at Rhythm’s MOMENTUM MC4R Pathway Summit also signal an effort to position the company as a reference point in this niche, alongside larger rare disease players such as Novo Nordisk and Eli Lilly that are active in obesity more broadly.

Advertisement

How This Fits Into The Rhythm Pharmaceuticals Narrative

  • The new diagnostic algorithm aligns with the narrative focus on better identification of rare genetic obesity, supporting the idea that improved diagnosis can expand the addressable patient base over time.
  • Heavy investment in education, consensus work and summit activities highlights ongoing spending needs, which ties back to concerns in the narrative about sustained losses and high operating costs.
  • The narrative discusses therapy development and market expansion in detail, but this kind of diagnostic infrastructure effort may not be fully captured even though it can influence future patient flow and market awareness.

Knowing what a company is worth starts with understanding its story. Check out one of the top narratives in the Simply Wall St Community for Rhythm Pharmaceuticals to help decide what it's worth to you.

The Risks and Rewards Investors Should Consider

  • ⚠️ Funding and supporting broad educational efforts can keep research and commercial expenses high, which matters for a company that analysts already flag for continued losses and possible future dilution.
  • ⚠️ Greater awareness and earlier diagnosis could draw increased attention from larger competitors in obesity and rare disease, which may have deeper commercial footprints and more diversified pipelines.
  • 🎁 A widely adopted diagnostic algorithm can help convert previously missed or late diagnosed BBS cases into identifiable patients, which is central for any rare disease focused therapy portfolio.
  • 🎁 Co developing the algorithm with global experts and patient groups can strengthen Rhythm’s position as a partner of choice in MC4R pathway disorders, which may help with physician trust and guideline inclusion.

What To Watch Going Forward

Investors may want to follow how quickly this BBS algorithm is taken up in clinical practice, for example through references in guidelines, use by multidisciplinary clinics and mentions at major congresses. The upcoming presentation at Rhythm’s MOMENTUM MC4R Pathway Summit and at ENDO 2026 will be useful signals on physician interest and real world feedback. Over time, disclosures around diagnosed BBS prevalence in Rhythm’s key markets, payer discussions tied to earlier identification and any commentary on referral patterns could help investors gauge whether this publication is translating into a broader, more clearly defined patient pool.

data.title: Is It Too Late To Consider Skanska (OM:SKA B) After Its Strong Multi Year Run
data.description:
• Wondering whether Skanska at SEK 250.40 still offers value, or if most of the easy gains are already behind it? This article breaks down what the current price might be implying.
  • Over shorter periods the stock is up 1.0% over the past week and 1.4% over the past month. Year to date it is down 2.9%, and over 1 year it has returned 16.3%, with a 94.0% return over 3 years and 30.8% over 5 years.
  • Recent news coverage around Skanska has largely focused on its role as a major listed construction and development company in Sweden, with attention on how it is positioned in capital goods and infrastructure projects. This background helps frame how investors may be reassessing the balance between growth potential and project related risks, which can feed into the current share price.
  • Skanska currently has a valuation score of 4 out of 6, which means it screens as undervalued on four of six checks. The next sections will break down what different valuation methods say about that figure, before finishing with a more complete way to think about what the stock might really be worth.

Skanska delivered 16.3% returns over the last year. See how this stacks up to the rest of the Construction industry.

Advertisement

Approach 1: Skanska Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) Analysis

A Discounted Cash Flow, or DCF, model estimates what a stock might be worth by projecting the company’s future cash flows and discounting them back to today’s value. It is essentially asking what those future SEK cash flows are worth in today’s money.

For Skanska, the latest twelve month free cash flow is about SEK 3.3b. Analysts provide explicit forecasts out to 2028, with Simply Wall St extrapolating further to build a 2 Stage Free Cash Flow to Equity model. Under these projections, free cash flow is expected to be SEK 8.5b in 2028 and, using a tapered growth path, around SEK 9.9b in 2035, all in SEK.

Discounting this stream of projected cash flows results in an estimated intrinsic value of SEK 364.06 per share under the DCF model. Compared with the current share price of SEK 250.40, this output implies the stock is about 31.2% undervalued using this approach.

Result: UNDERVALUED

Our Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) analysis suggests Skanska is undervalued by 31.2%. Track this in your watchlist or portfolio, or discover 209 more high quality undervalued stocks.

Head to the Valuation section of our Company Report for more details on how we arrive at this Fair Value for Skanska.

Approach 2: Skanska Price vs Earnings

For a profitable company like Skanska, the P/E ratio is a useful way to relate what you pay per share to the earnings that each share generates. It gives you a quick sense of how many years of current earnings the market is willing to pay for.

What counts as a normal or fair P/E depends on how investors view growth potential and risk. Higher expected earnings growth or lower perceived risk can justify a higher P/E, while slower expected growth or higher risk usually points to a lower P/E.

Skanska currently trades on a P/E of 18.0x. This is above the Construction industry average P/E of about 14.4x, but below the peer group average of 20.7x. Simply Wall St also calculates a proprietary “Fair Ratio” of 29.0x for Skanska, which reflects factors such as its earnings growth profile, industry, profit margin, market cap and company specific risks.

This Fair Ratio is more tailored than a simple comparison with peers or the industry average, because it attempts to adjust for Skanska’s own characteristics rather than assuming all companies deserve similar multiples.

Comparing the Fair Ratio of 29.0x with the actual P/E of 18.0x indicates that the stock appears undervalued on this measure.

Result: UNDERVALUED

Earlier it was mentioned that there is an even better way to understand valuation, so Narratives are worth introducing as a simple way for you to attach a clear story about Skanska to numbers like fair value, future revenue, earnings and margins.

A Narrative is your structured view of how the company wins or struggles, which is then linked directly to a financial forecast and, in turn, to a fair value that you can compare with the current share price.

On Simply Wall St, Narratives are available on the Community page and are used by millions of investors as an accessible tool to frame decisions, because each Narrative translates its story into forecasts that update automatically when new information such as earnings or contract news is added to the platform.

For Skanska, one investor might lean toward a more optimistic Narrative that is associated with a fair value around SEK 300, while another might choose a more cautious Narrative that is associated with a fair value closer to SEK 240. Comparing each of those fair values with the current price can help you decide whether Skanska appears closer to fully priced or offers a margin between price and your chosen story.

🐂 Skanska Bull Case

Fair value used in this bullish narrative: SEK 300.0

Revenue growth assumption: 8.46% a year

  • Bullish analysts model earnings reaching about SEK 9.5b by 2029, with a future P/E of 16.4x that is lower than the current sector P/E used as a reference point.
  • The upside case leans on Skanska keeping project execution tight while digitalization and modular construction keep costs in check.

🐻 Skanska Bear Case

Gap between narrative fair value and the last close at SEK 250.40: Skanska trades about 4.3% above this fair value anchor.

  • This view focuses on rising climate, regulatory and labor pressures that could squeeze margins and make large US rail and infrastructure contracts harder to deliver profitably.
  • Bearish analysts still see earnings rising toward about SEK 9.0b by 2029, but only supporting a lower future P/E of 13.8x.
  • The key question for this camp is whether contract risk, political exposure and higher compliance costs limit how much value investors should be willing to pay for Skanska.

data.title: RCI Hospitality Holdings (RICK) Q2 Loss Challenges Bull Narratives On Earnings Quality
data.description:
RCI Hospitality Holdings (RICK) just posted Q2 2026 revenue of US$68.7 million, with a basic EPS loss of US$0.04 and net income excluding extra items showing a loss of US$0.3 million, keeping the trailing twelve month figures at revenue of US$281.6 million and a net loss of US$6.5 million. Over recent quarters, total revenue has moved within a tight band between US$65.9 million and US$71.5 million while EPS has swung from a profit of US$1.01 in Q1 2025 to quarterly losses through late 2025 and into 2026. Investors will be weighing these headline results against what they imply for earnings quality and margin resilience.

See our full analysis for RCI Hospitality Holdings.

With the latest numbers on the table, the next step is to see how this earnings profile lines up with the widely held narratives around RCI Hospitality Holdings and where those storylines may need to be updated.

Curious how numbers become stories that shape markets? Explore Community Narratives

Advertisement

Losses Persist Despite US$281.6m In Sales

  • Over the last twelve months, RCI generated total revenue of US$281.6 million but reported a net loss excluding extra items of US$6.5 million, showing that recent sales have not translated into overall profitability.
  • Bears point to the 31.1% annualized increase in losses over the past five years and argue this pattern raises concerns about the business model, and the latest trailing loss of US$6.5 million keeps that concern front and center.
    • Critics highlight that quarterly net income excluding extra items has stayed in loss territory for three consecutive periods, from a loss of US$5.5 million in Q4 2025 to US$0.3 million in Q2 2026, which they see as consistent with the multi year deterioration.
    • The same bearish view links the Q2 2026 basic EPS loss of US$0.04 and the trailing basic EPS of US$0.78 in losses to a track record where earnings have not kept pace with revenue stability around the US$280 million level.
Bears who see the recent US$6.5 million trailing loss as part of a longer deterioration may want the full context of how that case is built and where it could break down 🐻 RCI Hospitality Holdings Bear Case.

Interest Coverage Stretched As Losses Grow

  • The risk summary flags that earnings do not cover interest payments well, which, combined with losses growing at about 31.1% per year over five years, points to pressure on the company’s ability to service debt from current operating results.
  • Bears argue that weak interest coverage can limit flexibility, and the trailing twelve month net loss of US$6.5 million against ongoing interest obligations fits with that concern rather than easing it.
    • What stands out to cautious investors is that even as Q2 2026 net income excluding extra items narrowed to a loss of US$0.3 million from a loss of US$4.7 million in Q1 2026, the broader five year trend of growing losses still supports the idea of strained coverage.
    • This same data set, which shows trailing basic EPS moving to a loss of US$0.78, is often cited by skeptics as a sign that interest costs rely on resources outside consistent earnings, such as past cash reserves or financing.

P/S Of 0.7x Versus DCF Fair Value

  • On valuation, RCI trades at a P/S of 0.7x compared with 1.8x for the US Hospitality industry and 8x for peers, while the provided DCF fair value of US$31.44 sits above the current share price of US$25.35, suggesting the price is about 19.4% below that model estimate.
  • Bulls argue that low multiples and a gap to DCF fair value point to potential upside, and the current mix of a 0.7x P/S and a share price below the US$31.44 DCF fair value heavily supports that optimistic angle.
    • Supporters often focus on the roughly US$6 difference between the DCF fair value and the share price, which they see as a cushion that exists even while trailing earnings are in loss.
    • At the same time, the contrast between the low P/S and the company’s loss making trailing EPS shows why others remain cautious, since the valuation gap appears alongside, not instead of, the US$6.5 million trailing net loss.
If you want to see how other investors connect this 0.7x P/S and the gap to the US$31.44 DCF fair value with RCI’s recent losses, it is worth reading the broader community take on the stock 📊 Read the what the Community is saying about RCI Hospitality Holdings..

data.title: Sun Life Links Stress Research To Growth In Employer Benefits Demand
data.description:
• Sun Life Financial (TSX:SLF) released new research on financial and workplace stress among Canadians.
  • The study links stress and money worries to sleep issues and broader well-being challenges.
  • Sun Life connects these findings to its employer-sponsored benefits offerings focused on employee health.

For investors watching TSX:SLF, this research ties directly to the company’s group benefits business, which targets employers looking to support staff health and productivity. By quantifying how financial and workplace stress affects sleep and well-being, Sun Life is adding more detail around demand for benefits that address mental, physical, and financial health together.

Readers can monitor how Sun Life uses this research in product design, client conversations, and broader employee wellness programs. The findings may also help shape future partnerships with employers and advisors that are trying to improve workforce resilience and manage benefit plan costs.

Stay updated on the most important news stories for Sun Life Financial by adding it to your watchlist or portfolio. Alternatively, explore our Community to discover new perspectives on Sun Life Financial.

See which insiders are buying and buying and selling Sun Life Financial following this latest news.

For investors, Sun Life’s research on financial and workplace stress looks less like a pure branding exercise and more like an attempt to underline the role of employer-sponsored benefits in long‑term corporate wellness spending. By framing sleep problems and well-being issues as outcomes of money worries and job stress, Sun Life is effectively reinforcing the case for more comprehensive health, dental, and financial-wellness coverage. That speaks directly to the group benefits segment, where Sun Life competes with insurers such as Manulife and Great-West Lifeco for large employer clients.

Advertisement

How This Fits Into The Sun Life Financial Narrative

  • The focus on health and wellness through employer plans lines up with the narrative that demand for health and protection solutions is an important driver of premiums and fee income.
  • If employers are slow to expand or redesign benefits despite this research, it could challenge expectations that wellness-oriented offerings will meaningfully support growth in group health and related products.
  • The research highlights sleep and stress as specific pain points, and that level of detail around employee outcomes is not fully reflected in the higher level drivers described in the existing narrative.

Knowing what a company is worth starts with understanding its story. Check out one of the top narratives in the Simply Wall St Community for Sun Life Financial to help decide what it's worth to you.

The Risks and Rewards Investors Should Consider

  • ⚠️ Employers may treat wellness and expanded benefits as discretionary spending, which could limit how much new business Sun Life writes off the back of this research.
  • ⚠️ Competitors such as Manulife, Great-West Lifeco, and large global insurers can use similar messaging, which may reduce any differentiation Sun Life gains from publishing this work.
  • 🎁 The research gives Sun Life a data-backed story to use in conversations with employers that are trying to address absenteeism, productivity, and retention through benefits design.
  • 🎁 Stronger alignment between Sun Life’s benefits offerings and employer concerns about stress and sleep may support deeper client relationships and longer contract durations.

What To Watch Going Forward

Following this news, investors can watch for concrete references to this research in Sun Life’s commentary around group benefits sales, renewals, and wellness program uptake. Any indication that employers are adopting products explicitly positioned around financial stress and sleep issues would show that the research is feeding into commercial outcomes rather than just marketing. It is also worth tracking how often Sun Life positions itself as a partner on employee well-being when compared with peers, and whether this theme shows up in future product launches or collaborations with advisors.

data.title: Genesco (GCO) Returns To Trailing Profitability But Q1 Loss Rekindles Earnings Volatility Debate
data.description:
Genesco (GCO) opened fiscal Q1 2027 with revenue of US$487.0 million and a basic EPS loss of US$1.42, while trailing 12 month revenue stood at US$2.4 billion with basic EPS of US$1.90. Over recent periods, quarterly revenue has moved between US$474.0 million and US$799.9 million, and quarterly EPS has ranged from a loss of US$2.02 to a profit of US$4.60, giving investors a wide set of outcomes to weigh against the latest print. With the share price around US$38.37, a key question now is how durable the recent profitability looks and what it implies for Genesco's margins going into the rest of the year.

See our full analysis for Genesco.

With the headline numbers on the table, the next step is to see how they line up against the most widely held narratives about Genesco, highlighting which stories fit the data and which ones the latest results start to challenge.

Curious how numbers become stories that shape markets? Explore Community Narratives

Advertisement

Trailing US$19.7m profit vs choppy quarters

  • Over the last 12 months Genesco earned US$19.7 million of net income on US$2.4b of revenue, even though Q1 2027 itself showed a loss of US$14.8 million on US$487.0 million of revenue.
  • What stands out for the bullish narrative is that this trailing profit arrives after several loss making quarters, yet bears point to the earlier five year earnings decline of 60.3% per year as a reminder that one profitable year does not erase a longer pattern of weaker results.
    • The bullish angle leans on the description of recent earnings as high quality and on trailing basic EPS of US$1.90. This view argues that profitability across US$2.4b of sales shows the business can cover its costs over a full year.
    • The bearish side counters that individual quarters still swing from a profit of US$47.5 million in Q4 2026 to a loss of US$14.8 million in Q1 2027. From this perspective, consistency remains a key watchpoint even with the year turning profitable.

Revenue growth at 1.5% vs US 11.8%

  • Genesco’s revenue over the last year grew 1.5% to US$2.4b, compared with a reference growth rate of 11.8% for the wider US market.
  • Critics highlight a bearish narrative that slower top line progress limits operating leverage, and the historical five year earnings decline of 60.3% per year lines up with that concern even though the company has recently returned to profitability.
    • The gap between 1.5% revenue growth and the 11.8% market reference suggests the business is not keeping pace with broader US growth, which bears link to pressure on long term earnings power.
    • At the same time, the move from trailing net losses, such as US$19.5 million in the year to Q4 2025, to a trailing profit of US$19.7 million shows that even modest revenue growth can still support positive earnings when costs are managed closely.

P/E of 21.2x vs DCF fair value gap

  • The stock trades on a trailing P/E of 21.2x, below the 46.1x peer average and slightly below the 22.3x US Specialty Retail average, while the share price of US$38.37 sits above an indicated DCF fair value of US$22.74.
  • What is interesting for a bearish valuation narrative is that the below peer P/E can look appealing, yet the DCF fair value being lower than the share price and the history of earnings falling 60.3% per year over five years give skeptics concrete numbers to lean on even after the company turned profitable in the last 12 months.
    • Supporters of the stock may point to the discount to the 46.1x peer P/E and argue that a business earning US$1.90 in trailing EPS on US$2.4b of revenue does not need to close that entire gap to justify current levels.
    • Skeptics instead focus on the DCF fair value of US$22.74 versus the US$38.37 market price, alongside the uneven quarterly pattern from a loss in Q1 2026 to a strong profit in Q4 2026 and back to a loss in Q1 2027, as reasons to be cautious about how much investors are paying for that earnings stream.

data.title: We Wouldn't Be Too Quick To Buy The Hong Kong and China Gas Company Limited (HKG:3) Before It Goes Ex-Dividend
data.description:
Readers hoping to buy The Hong Kong and China Gas Company Limited (HKG:3) for its dividend will need to make their move shortly, as the stock is about to trade ex-dividend. The ex-dividend date is commonly two business days before the record date, which is the cut-off date for shareholders to be present on the company's books to be eligible for a dividend payment. The ex-dividend date is important because any transaction on a stock needs to have been settled before the record date in order to be eligible for a dividend. Therefore, if you purchase Hong Kong and China Gas' shares on or after the 3rd of June, you won't be eligible to receive the dividend, when it is paid on the 18th of June.

The company's next dividend payment will be HK$0.23 per share, and in the last 12 months, the company paid a total of HK$0.35 per share. Based on the last year's worth of payments, Hong Kong and China Gas has a trailing yield of 4.9% on the current stock price of HK$7.17. If you buy this business for its dividend, you should have an idea of whether Hong Kong and China Gas's dividend is reliable and sustainable. So we need to investigate whether Hong Kong and China Gas can afford its dividend, and if the dividend could grow.

This technology could replace computers: discover the 20 stocks are working to make quantum computing a reality.

Dividends are typically paid from company earnings. If a company pays more in dividends than it earned in profit, then the dividend could be unsustainable. Hong Kong and China Gas distributed an unsustainably high 115% of its profit as dividends to shareholders last year. Without extenuating circumstances, we'd consider the dividend at risk of a cut. That said, even highly profitable companies sometimes might not generate enough cash to pay the dividend, which is why we should always check if the dividend is covered by cash flow. Hong Kong and China Gas paid out more free cash flow than it generated - 120%, to be precise - last year, which we think is concerningly high. It's hard to consistently pay out more cash than you generate without either borrowing or using company cash, so we'd wonder how the company justifies this payout level.

Cash is slightly more important than profit from a dividend perspective, but given Hong Kong and China Gas's payouts were not well covered by either earnings or cash flow, we would be concerned about the sustainability of this dividend.

Have Earnings And Dividends Been Growing?

Companies that aren't growing their earnings can still be valuable, but it is even more important to assess the sustainability of the dividend if it looks like the company will struggle to grow. Investors love dividends, so if earnings fall and the dividend is reduced, expect a stock to be sold off heavily at the same time. It's not encouraging to see that Hong Kong and China Gas's earnings are effectively flat over the past five years. Better than seeing them fall off a cliff, for sure, but the best dividend stocks grow their earnings meaningfully over the long run.

Another key way to measure a company's dividend prospects is by measuring its historical rate of dividend growth. Since the start of our data, 10 years ago, Hong Kong and China Gas has lifted its dividend by approximately 4.9% a year on average.

Final Takeaway

Is Hong Kong and China Gas worth buying for its dividend? It's been unable to generate earnings growth, yet is paying out an uncomfortably high percentage of both its profits (115%) and cash flow (120%) as dividends. With the way things are shaping up from a dividend perspective, we'd be inclined to steer clear of Hong Kong and China Gas.

data.title: We're Not Very Worried About Anterogen.Co.Ltd's (KOSDAQ:065660) Cash Burn Rate
data.description:

For the purposes of this article, cash burn is the annual rate at which an unprofitable company spends cash to fund its growth; its negative free cash flow. How Long Is Anterogen.Co.Ltd's Cash Runway?

A company's cash runway is the amount of time it would take to burn through its cash reserves at its current cash burn rate. In March 2026, Anterogen.Co.Ltd had ₩43b in cash, and was debt-free. Looking at the last year, the company burnt through ₩1.5b. That means it had a cash runway of very many years as of March 2026. Even though this is but one measure of the company's cash burn, the thought of such a long cash runway warms our bellies in a comforting way. How Well Is Anterogen.Co.Ltd Growing?

How Hard Would It Be For Anterogen.Co.Ltd To Raise More Cash For Growth?

We are certainly impressed with the progress Anterogen.Co.Ltd has made over the last year, but it is also worth considering how costly it would be if it wanted to raise more cash to fund faster growth. Companies can raise capital through either debt or equity. One of the main advantages held by publicly listed companies is that they can sell shares to investors to raise cash and fund growth. By comparing a company's annual cash burn to its total market capitalisation, we can estimate roughly how many shares it would have to issue in order to run the company for another year (at the same burn rate).

Anterogen.Co.Ltd has a market capitalisation of ₩272b and burnt through ₩1.5b last year, which is 0.6% of the company's market value. That means it could easily issue a few shares to fund more growth, and might well be in a position to borrow cheaply.
